meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with this beginner-friendly guide. Learn to use Git, Antigravity, and Claude Code to build your first professional portfolio project.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Use AI-assisted Git workflows to rapidly prototype, iterate, and deploy small, high-impact features. By combining Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you transform abstract concepts into functional repositories, building the portfolio pieces employers actually want to see.
content.intro:
The screen stares back, a blinking cursor mocking your lack of a 'big idea.' You have the technical skills, but the gap between 'Hello World' and a production-ready portfolio piece feels like an unbridgeable chasm. Every time you start, you stall, fearing your code isn't robust or your architecture is flawed.

But here is the secret: professional software engineering isn't about grand visions; it's about the relentless, iterative process of building and refining. By mastering Git as your laboratory and AI as your pair programmer, you stop being a student who can't find an idea and start being a builder who executes on them.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Scope
steps.description: Choose a specific problem—like a personal expense tracker—and use Antigravity to define the MVP scope to prevent feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I want to build a simple expense tracker CLI. Generate a project roadmap with 3 core features and a minimal file structure.

steps.title: Initialize with Git Strategy
steps.description: Set up your repository structure. Use Git branches to isolate features so you never break your main build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standard directory layout for a Python-based CLI tool, including a .gitignore for common secrets.

steps.title: AI-Driven Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the boilerplate code based on your Antigravity architecture plan.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the boilerplate code for an expense tracker that reads and writes to a local JSON file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate boilerplate code for expense tracking logic using local JSON storage"

steps.title: Iterative Commit Cycle
steps.description: Commit small, functional changes. Use Git to document your learning process, not just the code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the changes I just made to the data model into a concise, professional Git commit message.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m "feat: implement expense data persistence"

steps.title: Refactor and Document
steps.description: Review your code with AI to improve readability. A well-documented repo is worth more than a complex one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this code for clean code principles and suggest improvements for better readability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "refactor current code for better error handling and add docstrings"

pro_tips: Commit your AI interaction logs as a separate 'docs/ai-process.md' file to show employers how you think.
Use 'Git bisect' early to learn how to track down bugs in your own AI-generated code.
Always force yourself to write the first draft of the README before the code to clarify your intent.
Treat the AI as a junior developer: review every line it outputs before committing to the main branch.
Branching isn't just for features; use branches for 'experimental' UI changes that you might discard.
content.next_steps: Once your project is functional, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or GitHub Pages, write a blog post about the challenges you faced during development, and link it in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student waiting for inspiration; you are a developer with a live repository and a proven workflow. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
